title: Podman does not work inside systemd-nspawn (Error: openat2 `proc`: Operation not permitted)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman (rootless or rootful) is unable to run containers inside systemd-nspawn (rootful, no userns):

```
$ sudo podman run -it --rm alpine
Error: openat2 `proc`: Operation not permitted: OCI runtime permission denied error
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create and start a systemd-nspawn container with a GNU/Linux distro (`PrivateUsers=false`, cgroups v2, `SystemCallFilter=@keyring`)
2. Install podman, configure it to use crun
3. (optional) Configure rootless podman
4. `podman run ...`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ sudo podman run -it --rm alpine
Error: openat2 `proc`: Operation not permitted: OCI runtime permission denied error
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
$ podman run -it --rm alpine
/ #
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

- cgroups v2 both on host and inside the nspawn container
- nspawn configured to permit keyring syscalls (otherwise podman fails earlier)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version            
Version:      2.0.2
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.4
Git Commit:   201c9505b88f451ca877d29a73ed0f1836bb96c7
Built:        Sun Jul 12 23:46:58 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.0
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.19, commit: 25585dd8e8ebff68baa00f695115eff4191ca1f9'
  cpus: 32
  distribution:
    distribution: arch
    version: unknown
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: stratofortress.nexus.i.intelfx.name
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.7.0-pf4.2-1
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 15134556160
  memTotal: 67371970560
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.14.1
      commit: 598ea5e192ca12d4f6378217d3ab1415efeddefa
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: Unknown
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.4
      commit: b66ffa8e262507e37fca689822d23430f3357fe8
      libslirp: 4.3.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
  swapFree: 16608985088
  swapTotal: 17178886144
  uptime: 26h 21m 1.64s (Approximately 1.08 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  configFile: /home/operator/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: btrfs
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /home/operator/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Build Version: 'Btrfs v5.7 '
    Library Version: "102"
  imageStore:
    number: 1
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/operator/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 1
  Built: 1594586818
  BuiltTime: Sun Jul 12 23:46:58 2020
  GitCommit: 201c9505b88f451ca877d29a73ed0f1836bb96c7
  GoVersion: go1.14.4
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.0.2
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ pacman -Qi podman
Name            : podman
Version         : 2.0.2-1
Description     : Tool and library for running OCI-based containers in pods
Architecture    : x86_64
URL             : https://github.com/containers/libpod
Licenses        : Apache
Groups          : None
Provides        : None
Depends On      : cni-plugins  conmon  device-mapper  iptables  libseccomp  runc  slirp4netns  libsystemd  fuse-overlayfs  skopeo
Optional Deps   : podman-docker: for Docker-compatible CLI [installed]
                  btrfs-progs: support btrfs backend devices [installed]
                  catatonit: --init flag support
                  crun: support for unified cgroupsv2 [installed]
Required By     : podman-docker
Optional For    : None
Conflicts With  : None
Replaces        : None
Installed Size  : 89.08 MiB
Packager        : Morten Linderud <foxboron@archlinux.org>
Build Date      : Sun 12 Jul 2020 11:46:58 PM MSK
Install Date    : Mon 13 Jul 2020 03:32:51 AM MSK
Install Reason  : Explicitly installed
Install Script  : No
Validated By    : Signature
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Arch in systemd-nspawn on top of Arch on bare metal

comments.body:
This sounds like Seccomp on the nspawn container denying the `openat2`
syscall (I presume similarly to how it was denying the keyring syscalls)

On Sat, Jul 18, 2020 at 5:07 PM Ivan Shapovalov <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> .
>

I suspected as much, but specifying `SystemCallFilter=@keyring openat2` (or even `SystemCallFilter=@keyring @file-system`) in the systemd-nspawn config does not change anything.

Is there any sure-fire way to check if that's indeed seccomp?

Disabling it entirely on the nspawn side would work, but I'm not
familiar enough with nspawn to know how.

On Sat, Jul 18, 2020 at 8:43 PM Ivan Shapovalov
<notifications@github.com> wrote:
>
> I suspected as much, but specifying SystemCallFilter=@keyring openat2 (or even SystemCallFilter=@keyring @file-system) in the systemd-nspawn config does not change anything.
>
> Is there any sure-fire way to check if that's indeed seccomp?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub, or unsubscribe.

Looks like there is no way to disable seccomp, but it's indeed the problem. Specifically, libseccomp 2.4.2 (as well as 2.4.3 which is not yet in arch) simply does not support the openat2 syscall. Worked around by patching libseccomp.

All in all, not a bug in podman, thanks for assistance.

> Looks like there is no way to disable seccomp, but it's indeed the problem.

Just for the record, things have changed since your comment was written.
Systemd v247  (releaesed Nov 26, 2020) introduced support for the environment variable `SYSTEMD_SECCOMP`.

_If the $SYSTEMD_SECCOMP=0 environment variable is set for systemd-nspawn (and other programs that use seccomp) all seccomp filtering is turned off._

Reference:

```
[esjolund@laptop systemd]$ git grep -A 2 SYSTEMD_SECCOMP=0 v246
[esjolund@laptop systemd]$ git grep -A 2 SYSTEMD_SECCOMP=0 v247
v247:NEWS:        * If the $SYSTEMD_SECCOMP=0 environment variable is set for
v247:NEWS-          systemd-nspawn (and other programs that use seccomp) all seccomp
v247:NEWS-          filtering is turned off.
--
v247:docs/ENVIRONMENT.md:* `$SYSTEMD_SECCOMP=0` – if set, seccomp filters will not be enforced, even if
v247:docs/ENVIRONMENT.md-  support for it is compiled in and available in the kernel.
v247:docs/ENVIRONMENT.md-
[esjolund@laptop systemd]$ 
```
